name: Hate crimes
penalty: imprisonment up to 10 years and/or fine
statute_text: Whoever willfully causes bodily injury to any person because of the actual or perceived race, color, religion, or national origin of any person shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 10 years, or both.
summary: Federal law addressing hate crimes based on protected characteristics

name: Damage to religious property and obstruction of persons in the free exercise of religious beliefs
penalty: imprisonment up to 20 years and/or fine
statute_text: Whoever obstructs, by force or threat of force, any person in the enjoyment of that person's free exercise and enjoyment of religion shall be fined or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.
summary: Protects religious property and freedom of religion

name: Interstate communications
statute_text: Whoever transmits in interstate or foreign commerce any communication containing any threat to kidnap any person or any threat to injure the person of another, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 20 years, or both.
summary: Federal law regarding threatening communications across state lines

name: Firearms regulations
penalty: imprisonment up to 15 years, fine, or both
statute_text: It shall be unlawful for any person (with certain exceptions) to knowingly sell or otherwise dispose of any firearm or ammunition to any person knowing or having reasonable cause to believe that such person is prohibited from receiving or possessing firearms.
summary: Comprehensive federal firearms regulations and restrictions

name: Civil action for deprivation of rights
penalty: Damages, attorney fees, and injunctive relief
statute_text: Every person who, under color of any statute, ordinance, regulation, custom, or usage, of any State or Territory or the District of Columbia, subjects, or causes to be subjected, any citizen of the United States or any other person to the deprivation of any rights shall be liable to the party injured in an action at law.
summary: Allows civil suits for constitutional rights violations

name: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
penalty: Back pay, front pay, compensatory damages up to $300,000+
statute_text: It shall be an unlawful employment practice for an employer to fail or refuse to hire or to discharge any individual, or otherwise to discriminate against any individual with respect to his compensation, terms, conditions, or privileges of employment, because of such individual's race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.

name: Violence Against Women Act
penalty: imprisonment up to 20 years, fines, restraining orders
statute_text: The Violence Against Women Act (VAWA) provides federal civil rights protections and criminal penalties for violence motivated by gender. It creates a federal crime of domestic violence, sexual assault, and stalking.
summary: Comprehensive federal law addressing violence against women

name: Infringement of copyright
penalty: Damages from $750 to $30,000, plus attorney fees
statute_text: Anyone who violates any of the exclusive rights of the copyright owner shall be liable to infringement under law. For willful infringement, the court may increase damages to not more than $150,000.

name: Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA)
penalty: Back pay, benefits restoration, damages up to $122,000+
statute_text: The FMLA entitles eligible employees of covered employers to take unpaid, job-protected leave for specified family and medical reasons for up to 12 weeks in a 12-month period.
summary: Requires employers to provide unpaid, job-protected leave for qualifying events

name: Fair Housing Act
penalty: Damages, civil penalties up to $16,000+, attorney fees
statute_text: It is illegal for anyone to refuse to sell or rent, to refuse to negotiate, or to set different terms for the sale or rental of a dwelling because of race, color, religion, sex, handicap, or national origin.
